title: Human Review Calibration Loop
summary: Aligns reviewers around the same rubric before using human judgments as labels or release gates.
steps: Select calibration cases that include obvious passes, obvious failures, and ambiguous borderlines.
Have reviewers score independently before discussion.
Compare disagreements and update rubric language only to clarify existing intent, not to chase desired scores.
Run a second calibration pass on reserved cases.
Use the rubric for production review only after agreement is high enough for the decision being made.
stopConditions: rubric intent disagreement requires owner decision
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Human Review Calibration Loop for [rubric, reviewers, calibration_cases].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Select calibration cases that include obvious passes, obvious failures, and ambiguous borderlines.
- Have reviewers score independently before discussion.
- Compare disagreements and update rubric language only to clarify existing intent, not to chase desired scores.
- Run a second calibration pass on reserved cases.
- Use the rubric for production review only after agreement is high enough for the decision being made.

Quality gates before completion:
- independent first pass completed
- disagreement themes recorded
- rubric changes versioned
- second pass improves agreement

Stop when one of these is true:
- agreement threshold reached
- rubric intent disagreement requires owner decision
- budget ends

Finish with:
- reviewer score matrix
- disagreement notes
- rubric diff
- calibration result

Escalate for human approval before: changing business-critical quality definitions, using low-agreement labels for launch.
